subject: Active Funding Opportunity: $225,000 PAR-25-274 Grant for The Center for Mind-Body Medicine
body:
Hi James S. Gordon, MD,

I was reviewing The Center for Mind-Body Medicine's outstanding work, particularly your focus on how you specializes in clinical-level mind-body and trauma-relief training/intervention trials. In relation to active federal funding cycles, I wanted to ensure that this high-value funding opportunity was on your radar:

The federal program is: "Feasibility Clinical Trials of Mind and Body Interventions for NCCIH High Priority Research Topics" (Grant ID: PAR-25-274). The funding ceiling for this call is $225,000, and it is explicitly open to eligible 501(c)(3) non-profit organizations. The deadline for proposal submissions is November 17, 2026.

To assist your team in assessing this program quickly, we have mapped out the full eligibility parameters, historical match metrics, and direct application links on our live dashboard:
https://dalevirtualcoo-web.github.io/grant-feed/data/index.html

We also compile real-time deadline notifications. If you would like to sync active federal cutoffs directly with your team's Outlook or Apple calendar, you can subscribe to our free iCal feed:
https://dalevirtualcoo-web.github.io/grant-feed/data/grant_calendar.ics

If The Center for Mind-Body Medicine lacks the direct internal capacity or bandwidth to assemble the proposal package before the November 17, 2026 cutoff, our consulting team at Giles Grant Intelligence (GGI) assembles turnkey, high-scoring application packages on a value-play basis. Let me know if you would like to schedule a quick call to discuss proposal development.

Best of luck with your funding cycles this season.

Best regards,

Zac Giles
Managing Director, Giles Grant Intelligence (GGI)
grants@gilesindustries.com
